articles.title: Family Entertainment Group Names Nick DiMatteo SVP of Equipment Operations & Strategy
articles.summary: Family Entertainment Group, one of the largest FEC operators in the US, has appointed industry veteran Nick DiMatteo, ICAE, as Senior Vice President of Equipment Operations & Strategy. DiMatteo brings over 25 years of amusement and entertainment industry experience to the role. The hire signals FEG's focus on strengthening its equipment procurement, maintenance, and strategic planning functions.

articles.title: Family Entertainment Group Appoints Jason Clark as Chief Financial Officer
articles.summary: Family Entertainment Group has brought on Jason Clark as CFO, effective May 26, 2026, as the company enters a period of significant organizational growth. Clark's background spans restaurant, hospitality, and entertainment industries, making him a strong fit for FEG's multi-unit FEC model. Back-to-back executive appointments at FEG suggest the company is positioning for expansion or a major financial event.

articles.title: Semnox Deploys Cloud-Based Cashless System at Rebranded UK Amusement Park Adventure Coast
articles.summary: Semnox Solutions has implemented a fully cloud-based, cashless management platform at Adventure Coast in Southport, UK, completing the installation in under two weeks. The system includes POS stations, handheld terminals, and integrated payment processing as part of a broader venue refurbishment. FEC operators considering cashless upgrades will find this a useful case study in rapid deployment and operational modernization.

articles.title: Periscope Capital Invests in Amusement Connect, Signaling Private Equity Interest in FEC Technology
articles.summary: Private equity firm Periscope has made an investment in Amusement Connect, a software and technology platform serving the amusement and FEC industry. The deal highlights growing investor appetite for FEC-adjacent technology businesses. For operators, this infusion of capital could accelerate product development and support offerings from Amusement Connect.

articles.title: Ripley's Believe It or Not! Acquires Adventure Seekers FlyRide Attraction in Branson, Missouri
articles.summary: Ripley's Believe It or Not! World Entertainment has acquired Adventure Seekers and its FlyRide attraction in Branson, Missouri, expanding its family entertainment footprint in one of the country's top leisure destinations. The acquisition adds an immersive flying theater-style ride to Ripley's Branson portfolio. The deal is a reminder that branded entertainment companies are actively acquiring independent FEC-style attractions to build out multi-concept destination clusters.

articles.title: Family Indoor Entertainment Market Report Highlights Digital Attraction Investment and LBE Demand
articles.summary: A new market report indicates the family indoor entertainment sector is benefiting from increased investment in digital attractions and rising location-based leisure demand. The findings point to sustained post-pandemic growth in the FEC space driven by consumers seeking out-of-home experiences. Operators and investors can use this data to benchmark expansion plans and capital allocation strategies.

articles.title: Location-Based Entertainment Market Sees Worldwide Boom, Report Finds
articles.summary: A new industry report confirms that the global location-based entertainment market is experiencing robust worldwide growth, driven by consumer demand for immersive, out-of-home experiences. The findings are broadly positive for FEC operators as they validate continued investment in physical entertainment venues. Suppliers and investors looking to enter or expand in the LBE space will find the market climate favorable.

articles.title: Northern Lights Recreation FEC to Grand Open Adventure Golf & Sports-Designed Mini Golf Course July 5–12
articles.summary: Northern Lights Recreation FEC in Harbor Springs, Michigan, is hosting a week-long grand opening celebration for a new mini golf course designed and built by Adventure Golf & Sports. The course will serve as the anchor outdoor attraction at Rocky's Beer Garden, adding a revenue-generating amenity to the existing FEC. The project illustrates how FECs are layering outdoor attractions and adult-friendly hospitality spaces to broaden their appeal.

articles.title: Urban Air Adventure Park Prepares to Open New Location in Salem, Oregon
articles.summary: Urban Air Adventure Park is set to open a new franchise location in Salem, Oregon, with a sneak preview offering a look at the facility's range of trampolines, climbing attractions, and active play zones. The opening continues Urban Air's aggressive national expansion strategy. For competing FEC operators in the Pacific Northwest, this signals a new competitive entrant in the market.

articles.title: Inside Salem's New Urban Air Adventure Park: A Full Facility Tour
articles.summary: A detailed walkthrough of the new Urban Air Adventure Park in Salem, Oregon, showcases the brand's signature mix of trampolines, ninja courses, and active entertainment. The coverage gives FEC operators a useful competitive intelligence look at Urban Air's current attraction mix and layout approach. The Salem opening is part of the brand's continued push into mid-sized US markets.

articles.title: Slick City Action Park Opens at Potomac Mills Mall in Northern Virginia
articles.summary: Slick City Action Park, a slide-based indoor adventure attraction, has opened a new location at Potomac Mills mall in Northern Virginia. The opening is part of Slick City's broader expansion into retail mall spaces, a strategy that converts underutilized anchor square footage into active entertainment venues. FEC operators and landlords should take note of the slide-focused format as a differentiated alternative to trampoline parks.

articles.title: NRG Adventure Park Moves Forward at Southside Commons Development in Jacksonville
articles.summary: NRG Adventure Park is progressing with its planned location at the Southside Commons development in Jacksonville, Florida, adding another active entertainment venue to the growing market. The project reflects the continued trend of mixed-use real estate developers anchoring retail centers with FEC-style attractions. FEC operators in the Jacksonville area should monitor this new competitor's timeline.

articles.title: Tenpin Bowling and Entertainment Centre Opens New Venue in Blackpool, UK
articles.summary: Tenpin, one of the UK's largest bowling and entertainment chains, has opened a new entertainment centre in Blackpool, adding bowling, arcades, and family entertainment to the seaside resort market. The opening represents continued brick-and-mortar investment by one of the UK's most active multi-concept FEC operators. The Blackpool location expands Tenpin's national footprint and competitive reach in leisure-driven destination markets.

articles.title: London-Based Interactive Entertainment Concept with Virtual Soccer and Foosball Coming to Paris
articles.summary: A London-based indoor entertainment concept featuring virtual soccer, interactive games, foosball, cocktails, and fast food is expanding to Paris, France. The venue format blends competitive socializing with food and beverage, a combination gaining traction in urban entertainment markets. FEC operators developing adult-oriented or eatertainment concepts will want to monitor this format's European rollout.

articles.title: WildBrain CPLG to Represent Miraculous IP for Location-Based Entertainment Across EMEA and APAC
articles.summary: WildBrain CPLG has been appointed to develop location-based entertainment licensing opportunities for the Miraculous franchise across Europe, the Middle East, Africa, and Asia-Pacific. The partnership expands the popular animated IP's presence in physical entertainment venues such as FECs, trampoline parks, and themed attractions. FEC operators in these regions should explore Miraculous-themed partnership opportunities as the brand builds out its LBE strategy.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Partners with Toy Foundation for International Day of Play with Free Admission Offer
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese offered free Adventure Zone admission in celebration of International Day of Play, partnering with the Toy Foundation on a June fundraising campaign. The promotion is a strong example of how FEC brands can combine community engagement with philanthropic partnerships to drive traffic during the shoulder period before peak summer. Operators looking for cause-marketing playbooks can draw inspiration from this campaign.

articles.title: New Family Entertainment Facility Slated to Open in Springfield, Illinois in July
articles.summary: A new entertainment facility is scheduled to open in Springfield, Illinois, in July 2026, adding another FEC option to the central Illinois market. While details are limited, the opening reflects continued grassroots investment in community-based family entertainment. Local and regional FEC operators should track new entrants in their competitive landscapes.

articles.title: Chamber Members Preview Bear River Entertainment Zone in Fortuna-Eureka Area
articles.summary: Business leaders from the Fortuna and Eureka chambers of commerce toured the new Bear River entertainment zone, signaling growing local economic and community interest in the facility. The preview underscores the role FECs play in community development and local business ecosystems. Operators planning new venue openings can take note of the chambers-of-commerce engagement model as a soft-launch and community relations strategy.

articles.title: Floyd Bowling & Amusement Center in Georgia Proves Bowling Remains Serious Business
articles.summary: A profile of Floyd Bowling & Amusement Center in Rome, Georgia, highlights how the venue has built a loyal customer base around bowling as a core offering alongside amusement center amenities. The story is a useful reminder that traditional bowling remains a commercially viable anchor attraction when paired with complementary entertainment offerings. Independent FEC operators can draw operational inspiration from the center's community-focused approach.

articles.title: Adventure Golf & Sports Completes Mini Golf Build at Michigan Recreation Venue
articles.summary: Adventure Golf & Sports has completed construction of a new mini golf course at a Michigan recreation facility, adding to its portfolio of purpose-built outdoor attractions for FECs and parks. The project highlights AGS as an active supplier in the FEC attraction development market. Operators considering adding mini golf as a revenue-generating outdoor amenity can look to AGS as an established design-build partner.

articles.title: Six Flags Great America Launches 50th Anniversary Summer Celebration with New Entertainment and Experiences
articles.summary: Six Flags Great America is kicking off a 50th anniversary celebration running June 20 through August 9, featuring new entertainment programming, limited-time food offerings, and immersive experiences marking five decades of operation. Milestone anniversary campaigns like this are a proven strategy for driving incremental attendance and earned media. FEC operators can draw inspiration from the anniversary-themed marketing playbook for their own milestone events.

articles.title: Frostbite's Arcade & Creamery Relocates from Holts Summit to Fulton, Missouri
articles.summary: Frostbite's Arcade & Creamery, a small FEC combining arcade gaming with ice cream, has moved its operation from Holts Summit to Fulton, Missouri. The relocation reflects the ongoing challenge smaller FEC operators face in finding the right market size and venue footprint for their concept. Independent FEC owners navigating similar site decisions will recognize the strategic tensions involved in moving an established customer base to a new location.

articles.title: The Economics of Amusement Parks: What Makes the Business Model Work
articles.summary: A feature examining the financial dynamics underlying amusement park operations, including pricing strategy, capital investment cycles, and the levers that drive profitability at scaled attractions. While focused on parks, the economic principles discussed apply directly to FEC operators managing fixed costs, per-capita spending, and attraction ROI. A useful read for FEC finance and strategy teams.

articles.title: Pico Play Expands Vietnam Manufacturing Operations, Eyes Emerging Entertainment Markets
articles.summary: Themed attraction design and construction firm Pico Play is scaling its Vietnam operations to 200 workers by end of 2026, leveraging the country as a fabrication hub for global entertainment projects. The expansion lowers production costs and shortens lead times for operators in the Asia-Pacific region commissioning custom attraction builds. FEC developers and suppliers sourcing fabrication partners in Southeast Asia should monitor Pico Play's growing regional capacity.

articles.title: Arizona Ranks Its Top 10 Indoor Entertainment Attractions for 2026
articles.summary: AZ Big Media has published its annual ranking of the top 10 indoor entertainment attractions in Arizona for 2026, providing a consumer-facing snapshot of the state's competitive FEC and indoor entertainment landscape. The list offers useful competitive intelligence for operators in the Arizona market and illustrates which venue types and brands are resonating with consumers. FEC marketers can also study the ranking criteria to understand how their venues are perceived relative to competitors.
